description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running lengthwise across its entire width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, contrasting with the solid, untextured sides and base of the block. The stone appears to be a dense, dark material, possibly granite or basalt, with a slightly speckled, bluish-gray coloration on the grooved surface. The block is thick and robust, suggesting it is designed to serve as a heavy-duty barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. The grooves may provide additional grip or drainage, making it suitable for use in areas with pedestrian or vehicular traffic. There are no visible markings, engravings, or signs of significant weathering on the surfaces.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and solid, dark sides. The grooved pattern gives it a striped, textured appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block with parallel grooves running horizontally across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the block's thickness clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same grooved pattern across the top surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the lengthwise grooves and the block's substantial thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained with sharp groove edges.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
